entry.title: Improve Performance of TM integration of Sales Scheduling Agreements
entry.content:
Dear friends of TM,

Scheduling agreements cover the supply of goods from a supplier to a customer. It’s very common that they are valid for a longer period, often several years. That means that they are growing and growing in terms of their schedule lines, their document flow etc.

Now, there are means to reduce complexity, i.e. transaction SACO for sales scheduling agreements. But nevertheless, in TM integration this can create big challenges as we need to consider every single schedule line to calculate the open quantity still to be delivered, identify the transport requirements and built freight units.

Luckily, we meanwhile have some medicine for this headache. With release S/4HANA 2023 we released a customizing setting for purchase scheduling agreements, where customers can create rules to define which schedule lines should be considered during TM integration without loosing any functionality and still creating the correct freight units for the transport requirements. With release S/4HANA 2025 FPS1, we now also extended this to sales scheduling agreements.

Let’s take a look to what this means in detail.

Concept of the future horizon

At first, we need to ask the question which schedule lines are actually relevant for TM integration when looking into the future . If I’m the customer, I might send the releases to my supplier one year in advance. But when I’m responsible for parts of the transport…do I really plan those transports one year in advance? Maybe it’s only a couple of weeks in advance…and here the customizable rules come into play. With those, I can dynamically define how many schedule lines should be considered.

And dynamically means:

  • I define a timeframe in which schedule lines should be counted.
  • Depending on the count, I can define the horizon which should be considered during TM integration and thus how many schedule lines should be considered
  • Basic paradigm: The more schedule lines I count in the defined time frame, the shorter is the horizon of schedule lines to be considered. Or in other words: The higher the density of schedule lines is, the shorter is the horizon to be considered.

Let’s make it more clear with an example:

In the upper part, the density of schedule lines in the defined time frame is lower than in the lower part. Therefore, the end date for considering schedule lines in the upper part is more in the future.

Those rules can be defined for purchase and sales scheduling agreements.

Example

The definition of the rules are with the means of a rule group. This can be defined in customizing in the logistics integration part:

And the rule groups then are assigned to the logistics integration profile:

Now, the rule groups are considered every time the scheduling agreement is integrated with TM

Report / Transaction

There are also very rare cases where a scheduling agreement is not integrated with TM after a change (i.e. update with new releases).

For those cases we have created report /SCMTMS/UPDATE_INT_TIMERANGE where the new end date for considering schedule lines of a purchase scheduling agreement can be calculated.

This report has been extended for sales scheduling agreements and with some additional functions e.g., creating freight units:

For more information see also the release note.

So, with this innovation, you can manage your schedule agreements much more efficiently and you can improve the performance in TM integration a lot!

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Can co-products in SAP ECC have independent serial nu...
entry.content:
Your PP colleague is not entirely correct — co‑products can indeed have serial numbers in SAP. The limitation is not about the co‑product scenario itself, but about how serial number management is configured and used in production postings.

Key Points

Co‑product scenario (PP/CO):
You define co‑products in the BOM and routing. When you confirm production, the system posts goods receipt for the main product and co‑products proportionally. Costs are split according to apportionment.

Serial number management:
Serial numbers are controlled at the material master level (MM view). If a co‑product material is flagged as “serial number managed,” the system will require serial numbers during goods receipt, even in a co‑product posting. This applies regardless of whether the material is a main product, co‑product, or by‑product.

Traceability:
Each co‑product can have its own independent serial number profile. During production order confirmation (CO11N or Fiori equivalent), the system will prompt for serial numbers for each co‑product material. This ensures full traceability.

Where the confusion comes from
In some implementations, co‑products are treated as “joint products” without individual tracking, so serial numbers aren’t maintained. If serial number management is not activated in the material master, then yes, you cannot trace them. But technically, SAP does not block serial number assignment for co‑products.

entry.title: Join the Updated SAP Defense & Security Courses – Now Live!
entry.content:
Dear learners,

I’m pleased to share that the updated versions of IDS20_2601(61) and IDS30_2601(61) are now live and ready for learners. These new releases include fully corrected and revalidated hands-on exercises on the S4U SAP S/4HANA 2025 demo system, with master data and configuration aligned to the 2025 tenant.

These improvements ensure an even smoother, more reliable learning experience—perfect for anyone working with SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security scenarios.

Why Join These Courses?

Each course has been refined to match real-world Defense & Security processes and provides hands-on, scenario-driven learning. Here's a short overview:

IDS10 – Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

A high-level introduction offering conceptual grounding in SAP Defense & Security, ideal for learners looking to understand core capabilities, system navigation, and foundational processes.
➡️ Great for newcomers and those planning to progress into deeper topics. 

IDS20 – Exploring Organizational Flexibility in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

Designed for consultants and solution architects, this course dives into capability planning, organizational integration, HR topics, and accounting within the SAP D&S solution—supported by demonstrations and exercises.
➡️ Ideal for those involved in implementing or supporting Defense & Security structures.

IDS30 – Exploring Logistics and Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

A hands-on course dedicated to logistics, maintenance processes, and operational readiness in Defense & Security environments.
➡️ Perfect for practitioners working with material management, maintenance workflows, or operational logistics.

Start Learning Today!

Access on learning.sap.com (LSC): 

  • Defense and Security learning page
  • IDS10 - Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security
  • IDS20 - Exploring Organizational Flexibility in SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security

Greg

entry.title: SAP expands delivery of SAP Learning Class to meet growing needs of digital workforce
entry.content:
SAP offers learners a variety of ways for learners to consume content, from free access through to certification. One of the preferred ways to learn is using SAP Learning Class. This premium scheduled, instructor-led SAP training offering is delivered by SAP or SAP Authorized Education Partners and helps learners build practical SAP skills using real-life scenarios. 

Courses follow SAP-defined learning content and best practices, are delivered live by SAP-certified instructors, and are available in-person or virtually, with hands-on, certification-relevant content.

In 2026, SAP is expanding how learners access this instructor-led training by extending the partner-managed delivery model for SAP Learning Class courses, with a growing number of courses being delivered by SAP-certified instructors from partners.

SAP continues to own, author, update, and govern all course content and live training systems, while partners schedule, price, and deliver the courses. This expanded delivery model increases learner choice, flexibility, time-zone and language coverage, and course availability, while maintaining the premium learning experience associated with SAP Learning Class.

Expanding access to premium instructor-led training supports SAP’s pledge to upskill 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030.

Learn more about SAP Learning Class and the available courses.  

#SAPLearningClass #SAPLearning #SAPCertification #InstructorLedTraining #AIReadySkills

entry.title: Multistage Intercompany Scenario - Logistics Integration
entry.content:
Dear friends of TM,

Large enterprises usually contain of multiple entities, especially when they operate international. For instance, they contain separate companies for selling, delivering, and other purposes.

With S/4HANA2022, we already delivered the TM integration in the Advanced Intercompany Sales / Stock Transfer Scenario where the shipping enterprise had separate selling and delivering companies.

With S/4HANA2025 FPS01, we now also have the Multistage Intercompany Scenarios, where besides the selling and delivering company, multiple intermediate companies can be involved.

This blog posts talks about the respective TM integration of Multistage Intercompany Scenarios and its logistical aspects. Those are the freight unit stage building according to incoterms between the companies and also the updates to the outbound delivery with planned and actual dates from the supply chain.

Scenario Overview

Below is an exemplary Multistage Intercompany Sales Scenario with two intermediate companies:

Here, a customer places an order at the enterprise with the incoterm DDP which makes the enterprise responsible for the whole transport to the customer.

Within the enterprise, we have:

  • A selling company 1710 which sells the cargo to the customer.
  • A delivering company 1010 from which the cargo is delivered.
  • The intermediate companies 2210 and 2410.
  • The incoterms for the transfer of responsibilities between the companies are FAS (at the port of loading) and CIF (at the port of unloading).
Logistic Integration

The respective sales order looks as follows:

  • The customer is business partner 17100002
  • The incoterm DDP towards the customer is mentioned whereas the incoterm location is the customer’s address
  • The product is sold from sales area 1710 of company 1710.

The decision about the value chain and the affected companies is made on item level:

The cargo is delivered from plant 1010 of company 1010 with involvement of plant 1710 of selling company 1710. This defines the value chain.

The actual value chain is built with the sales and purchase orders as shown in the above scenario overview. So, after the initiating sales order towards the customer is created, the sales and purchase orders between the companies are created as well.

If the initiating sales order is relevant for TM, logistics integration collects the incoterms of the ‘Intercompany’ purchase orders, which are usually derived from respective info records.

And according to the incoterms and incoterm locations, TM then identifies the transport demand and builds the freight unit with its stages:

  • Pre-carriage from the delivering plant in company 1010 until the port of loading (in company 2410)
  • Main leg from the port of loading to the port of unloading (in company 1710)
  • On-carriage to the customer

In the picture above, transport planning has already happened with respective freight documents. With that, it’s clear when the cargo is planned to arrive at the incoterm location and thus when the responsibility is planned to be handed over from one company to the next one.

This is called ‘Transfer of Control (ToC)’ and the dates are stored as ‘planned ToC dates’ in the outbound delivery assigned to the sales order.

  • External Transfer of Control date for the delivery to the customer
  • Internal Transfer of Control for the responsibility transfer between the companies

When then the transport is executed, respective events (or transport activities) indicate when the transfer of responsibilities happened. These dates are again transferred to the outbound delivery as ‘actual ToC dates’:

The Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer Scenario is very similar. The only difference is that there’s no customer but a receiving entity within the enterprise instead.

Summary

Please see here a brief overview of the scope of te TM integration and the huge benefits customers have when they integrate this scenario with TM:

And here is the respective release note.

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Cannot Select Today for New Schedule
entry.content:
Good day @MuratTnrkt 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Is this a standard behavior where schedules can only be created for future periods rather than the current day?

Answer: Yes, it is. In Resource Scheduling, a schedule is a simulation until it is frozen. Any schedules that have not been frozen before their start date are automatically deleted. Because of this, the manual popup likely restricts start date selection to future dates to prevent you from creating a schedule that would immediately enter its start period (and potentially be deleted if you do not freeze it).

See the references below for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/3a8e781161f744fa8361014455b0fe9e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/f2d1705780175c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-business-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-asset-management/exploring-resource-scheduling_c29ff603-085e-4350-9c27-1037fe8e0f04-1

Regarding you point on “Also in the Generate Recurring Schedules app, the system allows selecting 'Today' as the start date with single, creates a schedule but it fails to show in manage schedule app;”

This can happen because the two apps are acting at different layers:

  • Generate Recurring Schedules validates the job scheduling input. An example is a Single Run that start today.
  • The generated schedule is still subject to RSH schedule lifecycle rules. This includes deletion if it not frozen before start date.

So, the behaviour you see is in line with a scenario where, schedule is generated, schedule period starts immediately, schedule is not frozen and schedule is then deleted.

Regarding you point on enhancement being a good idea to allow Today in manual schedule creation, you are right to be cautious about the impact on Freeze and Execution phase logic. Allowing Today in the manual create popup through enhancement could conflict with the intended process logic, especially around freeze timing, execution phase start and automatic deletion of non frozen schedule. This can create confusion for planners and destabilise your operational behaviour. Since there are no official details in public cloud on that, I recommend sticking to standard behaviour.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP Back Replication of Packaging Fees
entry.content:
This is a good and nuanced question because you’re dealing with two different replication targets in S/4HANA Cloud: material classification mapping (for attributes like kg of non‑recycled plastic) and pricing condition mapping (for amounts like fees/taxes).

1. Replicating kg of Non‑Recycled Plastic
This is already well‑documented:

  • You map the quantity attribute from the replication API to a material classification characteristic (e.g., ATNAM/ATWRT in the iDoc).
  • This allows the invoice form to print the aggregated kg per product.

Correct approach: Material Classification Mapping.

2. Replicating Plastic Tax (Total Fee)
Here you have two options depending on how you want to consume the data:

Option A: Material Classification Mapping
You can treat the TotalFee attribute as another classification characteristic. In the iDoc structure, this would be mapped into E1CUVAL / ATWRT (characteristic value) under the material classification segment. This way, the fee is stored as an informational attribute, not affecting pricing.

Advantage: Keeps everything in classification, consistent with the kg mapping.
Limitation: You cannot use condition techniques (like subtotals) because it’s not a condition.

Option B: SD Pricing Condition Mapping
You replicate the fee into a dedicated condition type (e.g., ZPLT for Plastic Tax). In customizing, you mark this condition as statistical (so it does not affect net value). It will then appear in the invoice condition list, purely for printing.

Advantage: Standard way to show amounts on invoices.
Limitation: Requires condition type setup and mapping in the SD integration.

3. Which Approach to Use
If the requirement is purely informational (no pricing, no totals):
→ Use Material Classification Mapping. Map TotalFee → ATWRT in E1CUVAL.

If the requirement is to show the fee explicitly in the invoice condition list (like other surcharges/taxes):
→ Use SD Pricing Condition Mapping with a statistical condition type.

4. Practical Recommendation
Many clients choose Option B (SD condition mapping) for fees/taxes because:

  • It integrates naturally into the invoice condition list.
  • It’s easier to print in standard invoice forms (conditions are already available).
  • You can keep it statistical so it doesn’t affect pricing.

Meanwhile, Option A (classification) is best for non‑monetary attributes like kg of plastic.

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
@alexey_arseniev, thank you for the comment.

I knew IF_SXML_READER/IF_SXML_WRITER, and now looked at IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER. But they are like "middleware" serializers, with which you need to write for every API data, ABAP mapping code. I checked also XCO_JSON in the past, an it is much slower even than /UI2/* one.

These serializations look more like transformations, where instead of maintaining transformations for each API type, you need to maintain ABAP code for mapping for each API type. And ABAP mapping will be even slower than tranformation.

Maybe I confused with "ABAP Kernel" term. What I wanted to mean is ABAP language implementation itself. I thought it is also ABAP Kernel, but maybe not, I am not sure.

Regarding ABAP types in "kernel". E.g. CALL TRANSFORMATION id can map dirrectly to ABAP types (maybe even generate references), but it supports only asJSON. The main question is why something like that cannot exist for "normal" JSON?

entry.title: Automated Test Suite: RHEL HA Solution for SAP S/4HANA ENSA 2 using Ansible
entry.content:
Introduction

Testing of High Availability Clusters used for managing SAP S/4HANA application servers is critical for ensuring their reliability and resilience. Many issues can be avoided in the first place during the pre-go-live stage by carrying out certain tests.

While manual verification of an HA cluster setup is possible, these tests can also be automated using Ansible. Developing custom roles and playbooks allows for a modular, reusable, and scalable design to automate each test case. This approach is particularly effective for tests like simulating a node failure to verify failover and resource migration.

Manual testing becomes tedious, time-consuming, and error-prone, especially in HA cluster environments where multiple nodes must be constantly observed during every test.

What is the Automated Test suite?

This Automated Test suite boosts quality assurance efficiency, test coverage, and accelerates bug reproduction/verification and post-maintenance, leading to faster production-ready RHEL HA cluster environments for ENSA 2.

The Automated Test suite requires a dedicated, out-of-cluster RHEL Ansible Control node to execute and monitor tests—including failovers and node crashes—on the SAP HA cluster. Using Ansible makes the tests transparent and easy to understand, and its built-in plugins allow saving logs for future review and audits.

Which tests are performed?

This automation test suite also covers the tests for the SAP HA Interface for SAP application servers. Each test carries out specific tasks to verify the cluster configuration and current state, conducts the actual test, verifies the output, and compares it with the expected results. 

The following table outlines what the test suite actually verifies on the systems being tested:

Test Name

Test Title

Minimum no. of HA Nodes

Test01

Name and version of HA software

2

Test02

HA configuration showing no errors

2

Test03

Shared Library (HA-Interface) loads without any errors

2

Test04

Manual move of ASCS works correctly with lock data

2

Test05

Irrecoverable outage of the Enqueue Server (ES) 2 is handled correctly

2

Test06

Outage of the Enqueue Replicator (ER) 2 is handled correctly

>2

Test07

ASCS moves correctly under load without specifying the destination node (Skipped in the current version)

>2

Test08

ASCS moves correctly in case of hardware or OS failure (Note: Node crash execution)

>2

Test09

Recoverable outage of the Message Server is handled correctly (if the SAP Profile Parameter “Restart_Program” is used for the Message Server) (Note: Backup and Auto-config)

>2

Test10

Irrecoverable outage of the Message Server is handled correctly (if the SAP Profile Parameter “Start_Program” is used for the Message Server) (Note: Backup and Auto-config)

>2

Please note that test07 is skipped in the current version and may be implemented and updated in future releases. Additional test cases are planned for inclusion in future releases to enhance coverage.

Prerequisites

1. A minimum of 2 node cluster configuration with ASCS and ERS resources to start with the initial tests, but 3 nodes are recommended.
Refer to the following document for the guidelines to configure the cluster that can be tested with the Ansible playbooks described in this blog: Configuring HA clusters to manage SAP NetWeaver or SAP S/4HANA Application server instances using the RHEL HA Add-On | Red Hat Enterprise Linux for SAP Solutions (Only ENSA2). Your pacemaker cluster should look like the following:

[root@s4node01: ~]# pcs cluster status
Cluster Status:
.....
Node List:
  * Online: [ s4node01 s4node02 s4node03 ]

PCSD Status:
  s4node01: Online
  s4node03: Online
  s4node02: Online

[root@s4node01: ~]# pcs resource status
……
  * Resource Group: s4h_ASCS20_group:
    * s4h_lvm_ascs20    (ocf:heartbeat:LVM-activate):     Started s4node03
    * s4h_fs_ascs20    (ocf:heartbeat:Filesystem):     Started s4node03
    * s4h_vip_ascs20    (ocf:heartbeat:IPaddr2):     Started s4node03
    * s4h_ascs20    (ocf:heartbeat:SAPInstance):     Started s4node03
  * Resource Group: s4h_ERS29_group:
    * s4h_lvm_ers29    (ocf:heartbeat:LVM-activate):     Started s4node01
    * s4h_fs_ers29    (ocf:heartbeat:Filesystem):     Started s4node01
    * s4h_vip_ers29    (ocf:heartbeat:IPaddr2):     Started s4node01
    * s4h_ers29    (ocf:heartbeat:SAPInstance):     Started s4node01

This is also a precondition before running any test.

2. Ensure that the SAP HA Interface for SAP ABAP application server instances is configured as mentioned here: How to enable the SAP HA Interface for SAP ABAP application server instances managed by the RHEL HA Add-On? - Red Hat Customer Portal.

3. Ensure that the sap.sap_operations collection for host_info and pcs_status_info module is installed on the ansible control node.

Getting started

1. Clone the community.sap_ha_cluster_qa repository and enter into that directory

# git clone https://github.com/sap-linuxlab/community.sap_ha_cluster_qa.git
# cd community.sap_ha_cluster_qa.git

2. Verify that the ansible.cfg, inventory and playbook files match your specific Ansible environment

3. Make sure that the inventory file contains at least the hostnames of all the reachable cluster nodes that you want to test:

For example:

# cat tests/inventory/x86_64.yml

---
all:
  children:
    s4hana-3n:
      hosts:
        s4node01:
        s4node02:
        s4node03:


 4. Ensure that the nodes are reachable via ansible ping, for example:

# ansible all -m ping -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml
s4node01 | SUCCESS => {
    "ansible_facts": {
        "discovered_interpreter_python": "/usr/bin/python3"
    },
    "changed": false,
    "ping": "pong"
}
s4node02 | SUCCESS => {
    "ansible_facts": {
        "discovered_interpreter_python": "/usr/bin/python3"
    },
    "changed": false,
    "ping": "pong"
}
s4node03 | SUCCESS => {
    "ansible_facts": {
        "discovered_interpreter_python": "/usr/bin/python3"
    },
    "changed": false,
    "ping": "pong"
}

 

How to run the tests

1. While in the community.sap_ha_cluster_qa directory run the playbook as follows for test01 to verify name and version of HA software.

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml  ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test01.yml -v

PLAY [Playbook to run test01 test case on ASCS and ERS instances] ***************************************************************************

TASK [Collect necessary gather_facts] ***************************************************************************
ok: [s4node01]
ok: [s4node03]
ok: [s4node02]

......

TASK [sap.cluster_qa.test01 : Print test results completing test01 test case for current instance] ***************************************************************************
ok: [s4node02] => {
    "msg": {
        "changed": false,
        "failed": false,
        "ha_get_failoverconfig_info": {
            "HAActive": true,
            "HAActiveNode": "s4node02",
            "HADocumentation": "https://github.com/ClusterLabs/sap_cluster_connector",
            "HANodes": "",
            "HAProductVersion": "Pacemaker",
            "HASAPInterfaceVersion": "sap_cluster_connector"
        }
    }
}

......

TASK [sap.cluster_qa.test01 : Print test results completing test01 test case for current instance] ***************************************************************************
ok: [s4node03] => {
    "msg": {
        "changed": false,
        "failed": false,
        "ha_get_failoverconfig_info": {
            "HAActive": true,
            "HAActiveNode": "s4node03",
            "HADocumentation": "https://github.com/ClusterLabs/sap_cluster_connector",
            "HANodes": "",
            "HAProductVersion": "Pacemaker",
            "HASAPInterfaceVersion": "sap_cluster_connector"
        }
    }
}


2. Similarly, you can run playbooks for the next test case by replacing the playbook. In this case test02.yml to verify HA configuration shows no errors

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml ./ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test02.yml -v

………
TASK [sap.cluster_qa.test02 : Print the results completing TEST02 test case for ERS node] ***************************************************************************
skipping: [s4node02] => {}
skipping: [s4node03] => {}
ok: [s4node01] => {

3. Similarly, you can run the tests for each test case by replacing the command with the corresponding test03 playbook as shown below. Test03 verifies the shared Library (HA-Interface) is being loaded without any errors.

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml ./ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test04.yml -v

Lock table data is compared and asserted 3 times in this test run: ASCS and ERS before move, ASCS before and after move, ASCS and ERS after move. 


5. Test05 for verifying irrecoverable outage of the Enqueue Server (ES) 2 is handled correctly.

8. Test08 to verify ASCS moves correctly in case of hardware or OS failure. Please note that in this test the primary ASCS node will be crashed using the “echo c > /proc/sysrq-trigger” command.

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml ./ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test08.yml -v

s4node01                   : ok=40   changed=1    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=1    rescued=0    ignored=0   

s4node02                   : ok=33   changed=0    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=1    rescued=0    ignored=0   

s4node03                   : ok=18   changed=1    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=2    rescued=0    ignored=0   

 9. Test09: Recoverable outage of the Message Server is handled correctly (if the SAP Profile Parameter “Restart_Program” is used for the Message Server). Note that this test will check for the “Restart_Program” parameter in the instance profile. If not found, it will be inserted, and the instances will restart before performing the actual test.

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml ./ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test09.yml -v
......
TASK [sap.cluster_qa.test09 : Display test summary] ***************************************************************************
ok: [s4node01] => {
    "msg": [
        "===============================================",
        "              TEST09 SUMMARY",
        "===============================================",
        "Message Server killed: 6 times",
        "Initial ASCS location: s4node02",
        "Final ASCS location: s4node02",
        "Initial ERS location: s4node01",
        "Final ERS location: s4node01",
        "HA Action taken: ASCS Restart on same node",
        "ASCS/ERS separation maintained: YES",
        "==============================================="
    ]
}

PLAY RECAP *********************************************************************
s4node01                   : ok=105  changed=1    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=77   rescued=0    ignored=0  
s4node02                   : ok=123  changed=6    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=22   rescued=0    ignored=0  
s4node03                   : ok=81   changed=0    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=64   rescued=0    ignored=0

Please note this test may take approximately 7 to 10 minutes since the message server is killed repeatedly up to 6 times or until HA software intervenes to perform a failover.

10. Test10: Recoverable outage of the Message Server is handled correctly (if the SAP Profile Parameter “Start_Program” is used for the message server). Note that this test will check for the “Start_Program” parameter in the instance profile. If not found, it will be inserted and the instances will restart before performing the actual test.

# ansible-playbook -i tests/inventory/x86_64.yml ./ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/playbooks/test10.yml -v
......

TASK [sap.cluster_qa.test10 : Display test summary] ***************************************************************************

ok: [s4hana17] => {

    "msg": [

        "===============================================",

        "              TEST10 SUMMARY",

        "===============================================",

        "Test: Irrecoverable Message Server outage",

        "Profile Parameter: Start_Program (not Restart_Program)",

        "Message Server killed: YES",

        "Initial ASCS location: s4hana19",

        "Final ASCS location: s4hana18",

        "Initial ERS location: s4hana17",

        "Final ERS location: s4hana17",

        "HA Action taken: ASCS Failover",

        "ASCS/ERS separation maintained: YES",

        "==============================================="

    ]

}


PLAY RECAP ***************************************************************************

s4hana17                   : ok=136  changed=4    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=10   rescued=0    ignored=0   

s4hana18                   : ok=119  changed=0    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=6    rescued=0    ignored=0   

s4hana19                   : ok=124  changed=1    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=1    rescued=0    ignored=0 rescued=0    ignored=0 

Refer to the README.md file of each test role for more details about the tests. The README.md for each test role can be found in the following location of the same directory:  ansible_collections/sap/cluster_qa/roles/<test-name>/README.md.

Authors: Amir Memon

entry.title: Re: Claude Code + Python + SAP GUI Scripting: Your AI Agent for Any SAP Transaction
entry.content:
Hi @nayan_kokare

you can try by yourself.

  1. Install Claude Code for Windows.
  2. Install python - by the way, Claude code can take over that part also
  3. let Claude code generate a sap script method library based on sap_gui_scripting_api_761.pdf 
  4. Enable Scripting (Alt+F12 → Options → Scripting)
  5. check RZ11 setting of your SAP system: sapgui/user_scripting = TRUE

Then log in into SAP system and try some prompts in Claude Code. You can start with "list my sap connection"

entry.title: Re: Dropdown is not showing for Dynamic Parameter, while...
entry.content:
Added CR for VS tag to your post, others are not required.

Go here and get the latest SP, CR for VS only supports the latest:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html?locale=en-US&ai=true

See my test app for setting Parameters and log on info:

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

Note, you must set a default value before setting the logon info so the SDK can query the DB for the values.

Note: Don't Verify the DB in code if it's not required.

entry.title: Re: Text in GOS note popup not cleared after calling cl_gos_document_service->edit_note
entry.content:
It works well in my S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 system with this simple program:

REPORT zdemo.

" Indicate any bank which exists in your system in transaction FI02 (country on 3 characters)
DATA(object) = VALUE borident( objkey  = 'FR 1000800001'
                               objtype = 'BUS1011'
                               logsys  = '' ).

MESSAGE 'In the next dialog, create a first note' TYPE 'I'.

NEW cl_gos_document_service( )->create_note( EXPORTING is_object  = object
                                                       ip_title   = 'Note 1'
                                                       it_content = VALUE #( ( line = 'Content 1' ) )
                                             IMPORTING ep_note    = DATA(first_note) ).
CHECK first_note IS NOT INITIAL.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

MESSAGE 'In the next dialog, create a second note' TYPE 'I'.

NEW cl_gos_document_service( )->create_note( EXPORTING is_object  = object
                                                       ip_title   = 'Note 2'
                                                       it_content = VALUE #( ( line = 'Content 2' ) )
                                             IMPORTING ep_note    = DATA(second_note) ).
CHECK second_note IS NOT INITIAL.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

MESSAGE |In the next dialog, edit the first note| TYPE 'I'.

NEW cl_gos_document_service( )->edit_note( ip_note = first_note ).
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

MESSAGE 'In the next dialog, edit the second note' TYPE 'I'.

NEW cl_gos_document_service( )->edit_note( ip_note = second_note ).
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

entry.title: Displaying a Hidden Field from the Compensation Worksheet in the Compensation Profile
entry.content:

entry.title: BTP BuildApps- Error handling and logging possibility
entry.content:
Hello community,

Currently,  we are developing Build Apps as mashup/extension for Sales and Service Cloud V2 or C4C V2.

Now we have use case to handle the address validation logic in Build Apps where the external APIs are consumed, that clearly means the APIs could give us different errors/exceptions in case any issue occurred from external system. How can we log and monitor errors occurred in Build Apps from external systems ?

Examples:
Network & Connectivity Errors: Timeouts, DNS failures, and connection resets.
Authentication/Authorization Errors: 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, invalid or expired API keys

Rate Limits and Quotas: Exceeding allowed request volume
Data Format/Schema Mismatches: API changes its data structure
API Provider Downtime (5xx Errors): 500 Internal Server Error (server crash) or 503 Service Unavailable (server overload)
Invalid URL/Endpoint Changes: The provider updates their documentation or removes an endpoint without notice

Can you guide us how to handle errors in Build Apps so that we can take advantage of logging and monitoring?
Thank you.
Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinstenzig

entry.title: Re: How to show Ramp-Up Coverage in SAP
entry.content:
Hi @Piteross1_ ,
Managing a ramp‑up plan in SAP—especially when the business refuses to reduce demand—can feel like trying to steer a ship with two captains giving opposite orders. The good news is that SAP gives you several ways to show reality without changing the official demand, which is exactly what you need.

I think, to Present a Realistic Ramp‑Up Plan Without Changing Demand in SAP
Your challenge has two parts:
1. Demand must remain high (management requirement).
2. You need to show what is actually achievable based on contracts, POs, and realistic supply.

SAP cannot magically reconcile these two, but you can use SAP’s planning tools to show the gap between official demand and realistic supply—without altering the demand elements. Here are the most effective approaches.

Use Stock/Requirements List (MD04) or Product View (RRP3) to Show Coverage
You can show management the coverage profile based on:

  • Current stock
  • Firmed purchase orders
  • Firmed production orders
  • Contracts / scheduling agreements
  • Supplier lead times

You will present it In RRP3, switch to the Pegging / Receipts & Requirements view and export:

  • Total demand per week/month
  • Total confirmed supply
  • Resulting coverage (days or weeks)
  • Shortages by period

This gives a factual, SAP‑generated picture of what the system can support.

entry.title: Re: Control Key YBM1 YBM2 S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
1. Why You See Two PR Lines
In Public Cloud, when you flag an operation with YBM2 (external processing), the system automatically generates a purchase requisition line for the operation itself. If you also plan services (expected value / overall limit) under that operation, the system creates a second PR line for the service.
This is by design: SAP treats the external operation as a “framework” requirement (the vendor activity) and the service line as the detailed procurement object. That’s why you see both.

2. Is This Standard Behavior?
Yes. In Public Cloud, two PR lines are expected when you combine YBM2 + service planning. The “operation PR line” represents the external activity. The “service PR line” represents the actual service procurement (with limits, expected value, etc.). This is consistent with the way the Manage Maintenance Orders app integrates with Purchase Requisitions.

3. Best Practice for External Services with Limits
Use YBM2 to mark the operation as external. Enter the service lines with expected value and overall limit. The PR line for the service is the one that will drive the PO and vendor billing. The PR line for the operation is informational and ensures the order structure is consistent. To connect a vendor contract/agreement number, maintain the source of supply (contract or supplier) in the PR line for the service. This way, the PO references the contract, and the operation PR line does not interfere.

4. Can You Suppress the Operation PR Line?
In Public Cloud, suppression is not supported. The external operation PR line is always created when YBM2 is used. In on‑premise, you could customize this behavior, but in Public Cloud the design is fixed. The recommended approach is to ignore the operation PR line for procurement purposes and work only with the service PR line (where you maintain limits and contract references).

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: Re: Max/Recommended number of SKU´s for RDP
entry.content:
There are no documented limits for the number of products and the system scales well even for large number of products.

Obviously calculation times for report data will increase with the increased amount of products, but remain within reasonable limits.

As most customers replicate the data during the night, the recalculation times are not a concern.

Some practical tips that your client can consider:

  • Do not replicate obsolete products, which are not used anymore (possibly the cross-plant status can be used for filtering out)
  • Do not replicate products, which will definitely be out of scope for the reports (e.g a product designed for specific market, where the market is not in the project scope)

entry.title: Re: Not able to see details in SAP ME for Customer Succes...
entry.content:
S-user does not have sufficient authorizations. The following authorizations are missing from the S-user:

  • the Partner - End Customer Sales & Adoption
  • the Partner - End Customer Info & Support

S-user needs to get the missing authorizations from SAP Partner Portal.

SAP Support does not support any topics or issues on the SAP Customer Success, SAP Partner Portal, end customer support, or the Partner Dashboards in SAP for Me.

Who to Contact?

  • Each SAP Partner has a Partner Experience Manager (dedicated SAP Contact) who can be contacted.
  • If you don't know who your Partner Experience Manager is, please contact SAP Partner Support using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard.
    Please Note- They do not work within the case application or via Expert Chat 

For the most current contact options for SAP Partner Support and the helpline, go to http://partneredge.sap.com and click? in the upper right corner (direct link https://partneredge.sap.com/en/public/get-help.html) or use the Get Help on the side of the portal

IMPORTANT!
SAP Support cannot resolve the topics listed in the Symptom section via case/chat and cannot assist with partner-related questions. SAP Support does not have access to the e-mail communication between a customer and Partner Support and cannot contact them for you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud, on AVC Need to derive BOM quantity...
entry.content:
Good day @Lord_Reaver 

Thank you for your question. See below.

Question: Is it technically supported in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to dynamically derive the BOM component quantity from a configurable characteristic (numeric, continuous value)?

Answer: Generally, yes, but the supported pattern is not to directly assign a formula key with a procedure such as ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD.

In Public Cloud, item category R is supported in BOMs, and variable size item quantity is calculated with maintained dimensions plus formula.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/8b76540d4fae4d119b9d74fe1c44a696.html?locale=en-US

Question: What is the correct modelling approach?

Answer: The usual modelling approach is:

  1. To use BOM item category R for the cable component
  2. Then maintain the size dimension length in M
  3. Maintain a quantity formula
  4. Map the configuration characteristic value (for example Z_LONGITUD) to the variable size item dimension using a reference characteristic or supported dependency target
  5. Let BOM explosion calculate the quantity and pass it to MRP.

The key point is that the formula key is part of the BOM quantity calculation setup, not a characteristic variable that you write to directly in a procedure.

Question: Should this be done via Variable Size Item reference characteristics?

Answer: Yes, that is the right direction assuming your tenant exposes the needed AVC/BOM mapping options. You normally map the configurable value to the variable size input dimension, and then the formula calculates the quantity.

Question: Is Quantity Formula supported?

Answer: Yes, for variable size items this is standard BOM functionality.

If you get a syntax error with ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD, check whether ROMS1 is a formula key, the dependency should instead assign to a supported dimension target, and the dependency is attached at the correct object.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Społeczności z powiatu SAPowiskiego spotyka poraz pierwszy w 2026 roku!
entry.content:
Nowe osoby w grupie

Dołączyło do nas kilka nowych osób, które wniosły świeżą perspektywę i sporo ciekawych pytań dotyczących rozwiązań AI oraz ich zastosowania w realnych projektach. Cieszymy się, że jesteście z nami. Liczymy, że poczujecie się tu jak w transakcji, która wreszcie działa – czyli tak, jakby ktoś włączył debuger i wyłączył wszystkie niepotrzebne user-exit’y.

Nowe podejście do rzeczywistości AI

Dyskutowaliśmy o tym, jak dynamicznie zmienia się rynek i w jaki sposób sztuczna inteligencja wchodzi do codziennych procesów: od automatyzacji, przez analizę danych, po nowe modele pracy konsultantów i deweloperów.

Wspólny wniosek był prosty: AI nie jest już dodatkiem do projektów, ale jednym z filarów ich realizacji. To wymusza nowe podejście do nauki, planowania i samego patrzenia na systemy SAP.

Co nowego w świecie SAP

Omówiliśmy m.in.:

  • nowe kursy i aktualizacje ścieżek certyfikacyjnych,

  • materiały przygotowujące do egzaminów SAP z elementami AI,

  • nowości w narzędziach wspierających pracę konsultantów, w tym rozwijane funkcje oparte na modelach generatywnych.

SAP coraz mocniej akcentuje połączenie klasycznego know-how procesowego z kompetencjami AI. To będzie jeden z kluczowych kierunków rozwoju w 2026 roku.

Plany na następne miesiące

Na kolejnym spotkaniu wracamy z jeszcze większą porcją wiedzy i dyskusji. Zapowiadają się sesje techniczne, prezentacje wdrożeniowe oraz przegląd nowinek, które już teraz kształtują przyszłość rynku.

Dziękujemy wszystkim za udział i zaangażowanie. Ten rok dopiero się zaczyna, a my już widzimy, że będzie bardzo ciekawy. Jeśli to dopiero rozgrzewka, to kolejne spotkania zapowiadają się naprawdę interesująco. 

Link do następnego spotkania: https://community.sap.com/t5/katowice-events/katowice-sap-stammtisch-15-04-2026/ev-p/14328630

Pozdrawiamy serdecznie!

SAP Cpmmunity Katowice

entry.title: A more applied, role‑aligned certification for SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce the reimagined C_TS422 certification, designed to help you gain real, end-to-end SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing competence by turning knowledge into project readiness and measurable operational impact.

The updated format better reflects day‑to‑day project realities and validates practical capability in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing. The certification remains relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on‑premise.

Why it matters
C_TS422 now emphasizes applied competence in realistic planning and manufacturing contexts, making it easier to identify professionals who can deliver outcomes. For consultants, it showcases the capabilities that matter on the ground: sound decision-making, understanding dependencies, and executing processes end to end.

What’s new
The exam is built around how work actually happens. It prioritizes using knowledge in authentic scenarios rather than recalling isolated facts, aligns to real consultant tasks across projects and operations, and expects an end-to-end view of how configuration choices, master data, planning and execution steps fit together and affect upstream and downstream processes.

What stays the same
The core domains and overall scope are unchanged. You’ll still be assessed on the fundamentals and key processes of Production Planning and Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. The certification continues to verify the core skills required for the SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing consultant profile and readiness to contribute in a mentored project role.

What you’ll learn and prove
Beyond topic knowledge, the certification validates how you translate requirements into working solutions. Candidates demonstrate application configuration, capture as-is processes and design to-be processes, blueprint effectively, and support change management. It also recognizes practical enablers of success through structured knowledge transfer, workshop facilitation, and Line-of-Business process expertise that drives adoption.


The certification covers the processes central to planning and manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA: Manufacturing Master Data Management, Production Planning, Capacity Planning, Managing Production Orders, Product Management, and Shop Floor Control. It reflects how functional design and master data decisions translate into reliable execution.

C_TS422 keeps trusted content but modernizes the way competence is measured. It highlights practical, role-aligned capabilities across planning and manufacturing, giving customers and consultants a more meaningful signal of project readiness and operational impact.

Get certified now: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-production-planning-and-manufacturing

entry.title: Re: KE30.
entry.content:
For your query 'I don't know where to check if the 'FI/MM quotations' include a profitability segment',

with the below transactions, you can check the profitability segment

FB03 (Display Document) → drill into line item → check the CO‑PA segment assignment.
KE24 (CO‑PA Line Item Display) → enter your document number → see if the posting created a CO‑PA line item.

If missing, the root cause would be Account assignment in FI/MM didn’t derive a PA segment (e.g., missing derivation rule in KE4I, or account not set up for CO‑PA transfer).

For your other query 'I don't know where to check if the cost elements assigned to the cost groups are correctly linked to the planned value fields'

check with the below transactions

KEI1 – Define PA Transfer Structure (cost element → value field mapping).
KEI2 – Assign PA Transfer Structure to operating concern.
KE4I – Assign accounts to PA transfer structure.

Verification Steps:

  1. Go into KEI1 and look at the transfer structure you’re using.
  2. Each cost element (or cost element group) should point to the intended value field.
  3. Cross‑check in KE30: the report reads only those value fields. If the mapping is wrong, the values won’t appear.

Tip: Use KEAT (Reconciliation) to see which G/L accounts are mapped and whether they’re flowing into CO‑PA. This is the best way to confirm alignment.

entry.title: Re: Devolução de Transferência
entry.content: Ola! Eu estou configurando um processo de Devolução de Transferência entre empresas do mesmo Grupo, seguindo a SAPnote 2453291. O processo de envio deu certo, o processo de devolução, quando vou fazer a VL10B para criar a Delivery, ele não considera o tipo de documento de vendas que criei cópia do DLR, ele insiste em considerar o DLR. Já conferi várias vezes a configuração e na teoria não faz sentido. Foi criado documento novos com base na SAP NOTE, limpeza de controles de cópia no que é necessário, etc. O único que se mantém o mesmo, é o Pedido de Compras criado cópia do NB, que atua tanto na saída quando na criação do documento de Devolução, tendo somente a diferença do flag de devolução no pedido, que faz com que ele traga o documento de remessa que criei para devolução e todos os outros documentos atribuídos à ele. Alguém conseguiu fazer este processo e se teve este mesmo problema, como resolveu?

entry.title: Re: Achieve Event Driven Architecture and FIFO Approach with Google PubSub Adapter in Cloud Integrat
entry.content:
@KaranRajwar 

Very well written blog regarding Google Pub Sub adapter.

However, I have to disagree with the premise that 'majority' of EDA revolves around FIFO. You are mixing Business Logic Validation with Message Ordering.

Your examples of 'Payment -> Shipping' and 'Order Processed -> Inventory' are Logical Dependencies, not FIFO use cases. In a decoupled architecture, Inventory service simply reacts to OrderProcessed trigger. If that trigger hasn't fired, the inventory doesn't change. Shipping service reacts to PaymentProcessed trigger or validation/gatekeeper in place: is Payment processed ? etc

Real FIFO use cases are much narrower - they are reserved for State Race Conditions within the same entity. For example, ensuring OrderCancelled event doesn't 'leapfrog' OrderCreated event during network retry or system backlog. That is actual lifecycle problem.

Using FIFO unnecessarily is overkill that turns an asynchronous system back into a slow, synchronous one. Even if you are very lucky and your Event Broker enables Partitions like Kafka or AEM/Solace, FIFO is still highly ops demanding and should be minimized at all times

Second aspect, worth to mention is that to establish FIFO, you need all parties (sender and receiver) to ensure FIFO, middleware is not enough.

br
Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Structured address data elements in ISO 20022 messages
entry.content:
@KHummel Dear Klaus,

our approach is to by default display only structured addresses in V9 trees. As you correctly mentioned, AdrLine nodes are still available, only deactivated. So if you require also the AdrLine nodes, you can simply copy one of the standard DMEEX trees and reactivate those nodes.

Same approach applies if you want to hide some of the active nodes.

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: STO Retorno - Transferência na localização Brasil
entry.content:
Olá Patrícia e Pessoal!

Estou configurando o processo de Devolução de transferência entre empresas do mesmo grupo, mercadoria em trânsito. Estou seguindo a orientação sap pnotes 2453291

Fiz a configuração conforme orientação e estou tendo problema no momento de criar a VL10B da Devolução, onde insiste em ter como referência o tipo de ordem de venda DLR do standard ao invés de buscar o tipo de ordem de vendas que criei cópia do DLR e do qual tem toda a atribuição realizada em todos os documentos que foram criados, conforme orientação da SAP Notes. Se eu coloco no controle de cópia VTLA o DLR para o documento de remessa cópia do NCR, ele cria a remessa de devolução, porém, me dá problema na ABA Brasil onde traz toda a parte de dispositivo lega, código de impostos e CFOP. No debug vimos que ele busca o NFTYPE, que no caso estou considerando o que tenho criado de devolução, mas ele busca o DLR, que não foi configurado aqui. Alguém já passou por isso? Ou conseguiu fazer funcionar este processo?

entry.title: GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statutory Reports" app
entry.content:
GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statutory Reports" app


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statuto...
entry.content:
Dear all,

The GSTR-6 report provides details of all documents issued for the distribution of input tax credit, supplier invoices received by the ISD unit, and the manner in which the credit is distributed, including the related tax invoices. This monthly return must be filed by every Input Service Distributor (ISD). It contains information about the Input Tax Credit (ITC) received by the ISD and its subsequent distribution. Filing GSTR-6 is mandatory for all ISDs, even if there are no transactions (Nil return).

 

The source document types and the relevant data required for GSTR6A consistency checks have been defined. These will also be treated as source types for GSTR6 reporting purposes. Please refer the below help document to configuration for key users,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/d977d2cab26c45f2aa0d26a749955629.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

Vinu Nanjan

entry.title: Re: Instruction Key Mapping to Payment Media File
entry.content:
Dear @thomas_janssen ,

If you are on S/4 HANA you have the fields CODE1-4, TEXT1-4 and ADD_INFO1-4 in the structure FPAYHX that read the attributes of particular Instructions. If you are on ECC, you need to read them yourself.

If we are speaking about our CGI payments formats, we already do read Instructions also in our solution for ECC. We use Instruction 1 for Service Level Code, Instruction 2 for Category Purpose Code and Instruction 3 for Local Instrument Code. The logic for Local Instrument Code is rather new, so you may need to download the latest notes as per our collective CGI notes depending on your system version:

S/4: https://me.sap.com/notes/2784858/E

ECC: https://me.sap.com/notes/2253571/E

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?
entry.content:
Hi Frederic, 

did you checked 

195442 - Language import and Support Packages 

352941 - Consultation: Languages and Support Packages

 

Importing a patch after language supplementation

If you have supplemented a language and then imported Support Packages, there is a risk of supplemented texts being deleted again. This especially affects languages that are rarely translated.
For this reason we recommend you repeat the language supplementation after you have imported a Support Package. A language supplementation in client 000 (client-independent tables) should be sufficient.

 

in your screenshot is shown that your supplementation language (english) has some warnings (yellow triangle).

furthermore, did you observe this behaviour in every client? 

best regards
